title: Query Optimization
objectives: Read EXPLAIN plans and spot problems
Rewrite queries to use indexes and aggregates

sections.markdown: Query optimization is the practice of shaping SQL and schemas so the planner picks efficient plans — index scans instead of sequential scans, hash joins with correct build sides, streaming aggregates instead of sorts.

sections.code:
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS)
SELECT o.id, u.name
FROM orders o JOIN users u ON u.id = o.user_id
WHERE o.status = 'PAID' AND o.created_at > now() - interval '7 days'
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC
LIMIT 20;

-- Fix: index (status, created_at DESC) on orders

sections.markdown: 'Show me the slow query' → run EXPLAIN ANALYZE, look for **Seq Scan** on big tables, **Sort** without an ordered index, and **Rows Removed by Filter** (predicate not pushed down). Rewrites often beat re-indexes.

OFFSET pagination on large tables — the DB scans and discards N rows for each page. Use **keyset pagination**: `WHERE (created_at, id) < (?, ?) ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT 20` — O(log n) per page.
Enable pg_stat_statements / Performance Schema. Rewrite `SELECT *` to specific columns. Batch fetches with IN(?, ?, ?, …) to kill N+1. Cache read-heavy aggregates or use materialized views.

flashcards.front: First tool for a slow query?
flashcards.back: EXPLAIN ANALYZE (or the equivalent) — read the actual plan.

flashcards.front: Why avoid OFFSET on large tables?
flashcards.back: The DB scans OFFSET+LIMIT rows on every page; use keyset (cursor) pagination instead.

flashcards.front: How to eliminate N+1 in the app?
flashcards.back: Batch fetches (IN clauses) or single JOIN; avoid per-row queries in loops.

quiz.q: For paging huge result sets, prefer…
quiz.explain: Keyset pagination is O(log n) per page.

quiz.q: 'Seq Scan' on a hot large table usually means…
quiz.explain: Add or fix the index to enable an index scan.

quiz.q: Best way to kill an app-side N+1…
options: Batch fetch with IN() or JOIN
quiz.explain: One query for many rows beats N per-row queries.

challenges.text: EXPLAIN a query; identify Seq Scan vs Index Scan.
Convert OFFSET pagination to keyset pagination.
Rewrite a subquery to a JOIN and confirm faster plan.
Add a materialized view + refresh strategy for a heavy aggregate.

interviewQuestions: Walk through reading an EXPLAIN ANALYZE plan.
How do you fix an N+1 problem?
Why is OFFSET pagination bad on large tables?
How do you find slow queries in production?
When would you use a materialized view?
